questions.text: The breaking of C-C bonds of complex compounds through oxidation within the cells is called:
questions.explanation: Respiration releases energy used for synthesizing ATP.

questions.text: Glycolysis takes place in the:
questions.explanation: Glycolysis occurs in the cytosol/cytoplasm of all living cells.

questions.text: The scheme of glycolysis was given by:
questions.explanation: It is also known as the EMP pathway.

questions.text: In glycolysis, one molecule of glucose is broken down into two molecules of:
questions.explanation: Glucose (6C) is converted to 2 Pyruvic acid (3C).

questions.text: What is the net gain of ATP molecules during glycolysis?
questions.explanation: 4 ATP are produced, but 2 are consumed in the preparatory phase.

questions.text: The enzyme that converts glucose to glucose-6-phosphate is:
questions.explanation: This is the first step of glycolysis, requiring one ATP.

questions.text: Fermentation takes place under ______ conditions.
questions.explanation: It is the incomplete oxidation of glucose in the absence of oxygen.

questions.text: In yeast fermentation, pyruvic acid is converted into:
questions.explanation: This is called alcoholic fermentation.

questions.text: TCA cycle (Krebs cycle) takes place in the:
questions.explanation: Named after Hans Krebs who elucidated the cycle.

questions.text: The 'link reaction' between glycolysis and Krebs cycle produces:
questions.explanation: Oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate forms Acetyl CoA.

questions.text: The first member of the Krebs cycle is:
questions.explanation: Citric acid is formed by condensation of Acetyl CoA with OAA.

questions.text: How many molecules of CO2 are released in one turn of the Krebs cycle?
questions.explanation: Released during the conversion of isocitrate to alpha-ketoglutarate and alpha-ketoglutarate to succinyl-CoA.

questions.text: The Respiratory Quotient (RQ) for carbohydrates is:
questions.explanation: RQ = Vol of CO2 evolved / Vol of O2 consumed. For glucose, it is 6/6 = 1.

questions.text: The electron transport system (ETS) is located in the:
questions.explanation: ETS consists of five complexes embedded in the cristae.

questions.text: The final electron acceptor in the ETS is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen combines with electrons and protons to form water.

questions.text: Oxidative phosphorylation refers to the formation of ATP using energy from:
questions.explanation: It happens at Complex V (ATP synthase).

questions.text: For each molecule of NADH oxidized in ETS, how many molecules of ATP are produced?
questions.explanation: FADH2 oxidation yields only two ATP molecules.

questions.text: The RQ of fats (like Tripalmitin) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Fats require more oxygen for oxidation, making the ratio less than 1.

questions.text: Complex IV in the ETS is:
questions.explanation: It contains cytochromes a and a3.

questions.text: Respiration is an ______ pathway because it involves both breakdown and synthesis.
questions.explanation: Intermediates of the cycle are withdrawn for synthesizing other compounds.

questions.text: The total number of ATP produced from one molecule of glucose in aerobic respiration is:
questions.explanation: Theoretical maximum yield including glycolysis, Krebs, and ETS.

questions.text: Which of the following acts as a mobile electron carrier in ETS?
questions.explanation: Ubiquinone moves within the membrane; Cytochrome c is on the outer surface of the inner membrane.

questions.text: F1 headpiece of ATP synthase is the site for:
questions.explanation: F0 is the integral membrane protein that acts as a channel.

questions.text: Lactic acid fermentation occurs in:
questions.explanation: Occurs when oxygen supply is inadequate for aerobic respiration.

questions.text: The gain of ATP in fermentation is very low, about ______% of energy in glucose.
questions.explanation: Less than 7% of the energy is trapped as high-energy bonds of ATP.

questions.text: Pyruvate decarboxylase and alcohol dehydrogenase are enzymes used in:
questions.explanation: Used by yeast to convert pyruvic acid to ethanol.

questions.text: When proteins are used as respiratory substrate, the RQ is:
questions.explanation: Typical value for proteins.

questions.text: Cytochromes are found in:
questions.explanation: Inner mitochondrial membrane folds (cristae) contain the respiratory chain.

questions.text: Which compound is the 'hub' or 'common pathway' for carbohydrates, fats and proteins?
questions.explanation: Most substrates are converted to Acetyl CoA before entering the Krebs cycle.

questions.text: GTP is produced during the conversion of:
questions.explanation: This is a substrate-level phosphorylation step in the Krebs cycle.

questions.text: Aerobic respiration is ______ efficient than fermentation.
questions.explanation: Aerobic respiration yields 38 ATP vs 2 ATP in fermentation.

questions.text: Plants can survive without respiratory organs because:
options: Each plant part takes care of its own needs
Most cells are close to the surface
questions.explanation: Plants do not have a specialized system like the lungs in animals.

questions.text: In the Krebs cycle, NAD+ is reduced to NADH at how many points?
questions.explanation: Three steps of NADH formation and one of FADH2 formation.

questions.text: During which step of glycolysis is NADH + H+ produced?
questions.explanation: Oxidation of 3-phosphoglyceraldehyde to 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate.

questions.text: The end products of aerobic respiration are:
questions.explanation: Complete oxidation results in inorganic waste and chemical energy.

questions.text: The energy currency of the cell is:
questions.explanation: Adenosine Triphosphate.

questions.text: Water is formed in ETS at:
questions.explanation: Cytochrome c oxidase transfers electrons to Oxygen to form water.

questions.text: Which of these are respiratory substrates?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are preferred, but others can be used.

questions.text: Sucrose is converted into glucose and fructose by the enzyme:
questions.explanation: This allows complex sugars to enter the glycolytic pathway.

questions.text: Oxygen is used only in the ______ stage of the respiratory process.
questions.explanation: Oxygen acts as the final hydrogen acceptor in the ETS.
